info.name: LMS — Switch Account (v1)
info.description:
Multi-profile account switching for the mobile app.

**Model:** the app keeps a local list of "added accounts". Each added account is a `{ token, card }` pair returned by **Add Account**. Switching between profiles (e.g. a Student account and a Teacher account on the same device) is done **client-side** by selecting which stored token to send — no server round-trip is needed. The endpoints here let the app add, remove and refresh those accounts.

**Profile card** (the `data.snapshot` object) always contains the fields the switcher UI needs:
- `image` — profile picture URL
- `username` — display name
- `user_type` — `student` or `teacher`
(plus `email`, `organization`, and `class_info` for extra context).

**Auth:** Sanctum bearer token. `Add Account` is public and returns a token; the other endpoints require the token of the account being acted on. The Add requests auto-save the returned token into the `authToken` environment variable.

request.description: Add a STUDENT account. Pass `identifier` (admission number) + `password`; the role is auto-detected (no `login_type`). Returns `{ account, token, token_type }`; `account.user_type` will be `student`.

Add a TEACHER / ADMIN / SUB-ADMIN / ACCOUNTS account by email + password. Pass `identifier` (email) + `password`; the role is auto-detected (no `login_type`). `account.user_type` ∈ `teacher | admin | accounts`.

Returns the latest profile card for the token in the Authorization header. The app calls this to refresh a stored account's `image` / `username` / `user_type`. Set `authToken` to the account you want to refresh.
Revokes ONLY the token in the Authorization header (the account being removed from the device's switcher). Other added accounts keep working. Set `authToken` to the account you want to remove.

Auto-discovery: lists every active account that shares the current user's email + role across organizations (for users enrolled in multiple schools with one email). Returns each with `is_current` and its `school`.

exec: console.log('Switched — saved new token to authToken');

request.description: Revokes the current token and issues a new one for the SAME email + role in a different organization (`organization_id`). Used by the linked-schools flow above. Returns `{ user, token, token_type }`.
